prompt: This method fails to compile with 'missing return statement'. What is the bug?
choices: The method should be declared void since not all branches produce a value
The compiler cannot prove all paths return — add return "zero"; after the if/else-if block
else if should be changed to else to cover all remaining cases
Java requires a default return at the top of every non-void method
explanation: The compiler requires every path through a non-void method to end with a return. If n == 0, neither the if nor the else-if runs, leaving execution with no return value at the end of the method. Fix: add return "zero"; after the if/else-if, or use an else block instead.
